pilot: Gide2019 PRE, de-novo Salmon quant (transcriptome index, 3M read-pairs/sample subsample)

headline.interpretation: A de-novo transcriptomic antigen-presentation axis, computed entirely from raw reads, perfectly and cross-validated-separates ICB responders from non-responders in this balanced pilot.
headline.loo_method: fold-contained: panel z-standardization refit on TRAIN samples only within each leave-one-out fold (no test-sample leakage into scaling); logistic map also fit per fold
headline.note_standardization: An earlier version z-scored the panel over all 12 samples before the LOO split (mild standardization leakage). Recomputed with fold-contained standardization: 3-gene axis LOO=1.00 (unchanged), 6-gene panel LOO=0.917. Headline is robust to the fix.

caveats: n=12 pilot; a perfect separator warrants replication in the full cohort.
Transcriptome-only index + 3M-read subsample: sufficient for gene-level programs, NOT for the WES-blind phenotypes (IR, editing/AEI, TE-locus) that require the sibling pipeline's genome alignment.
Immune-axis signal may partly reflect immune-cell infiltration / purity; tumor-intrinsic vs microenvironment decomposition needs deconvolution (deferred to full analysis).
This pilot proves the read-level machinery + that de-novo RNA features organize response; it is the positive counterpart to the null WES-proxy internal test.
LOO is now fold-contained (train-only standardization); the earlier all-sample z-scoring did not inflate the 3-gene headline but is corrected here for rigor.
